current_suggestion.pitch: Russ Roberts hosts long-form conversations that sit at the intersection of economics, philosophy, and how to live well — territory your channels only graze. Where Ben Felix covers personal finance and Peter Attia covers longevity, EconTalk steelmans the economic and moral reasoning underneath decisions about tradeoffs, uncertainty, and human flourishing, often drawing on Hayek, Adam Smith's Theory of Moral Sentiments, and Stoic-adjacent ideas. Episodes are single-guest, 60-90 minutes, and tend toward genuine intellectual humility rather than thesis-defending — the format rewards the same careful listening that poker theory and Lex Fridman content demands.
